title: Game Boundary Design
use_for: isolating Unity game APIs, private state, hooks, reflection, and host mutations from domain logic
designing hook, probe, facade, adapter, and mutation-scope roles
making game integration readiness and failure visible
preventing stale host references from leaking into mechanics
do_not_use_for: pure domain modeling with no external game dependency
rules: hooks should capture events, not run feature policy
probes should discover or sample host state, not own domain decisions
facades should expose stable contracts over fragile host APIs
mutation scopes should make external writes explicit and gated
all fragile integrations need readiness and degradation diagnostics
procedure: List required host interactions: notifications, reads, writes, UI insertion, assets, networking, or timing.
Classify each interaction as hook, probe, facade, adapter, mutation, or surface.
Define stable snapshots or contracts exposed to modules and domain logic.
Define invalidation rules for scene, entity, session, or lifecycle changes.
Define readiness checks and degraded behavior for missing or changed host members.
Verify domain logic cannot reach raw host objects unless explicitly allowed.

primary_when: the task is mainly about isolating or designing access to game APIs and host state
must_not_own_when: the task is only about internal domain pipeline stages
the task is only about generic module lifecycle

solves: how to stop raw game integration from contaminating domain architecture
how to make fragile host interactions observable and degradable
how to define stable access contracts for modules
does_not_solve: the internal mechanics of a feature

core_principles: The host game is an external system, even when its objects are in process.
Stable snapshots and facades are safer than passing live host objects inward.
Mutation should be rarer and more explicit than reading.
Fragility must be represented as runtime state, not surprise exceptions.
constraints: Do not let domain mechanics subscribe directly to hooks.
Do not expose reflection results as permanent domain state.
Do not mutate host state before lifecycle readiness is true.
Do not ignore missing host members; report degraded integration.
decision_rules: If an interaction is event-like, model it as a hook that publishes an internal event.
If an interaction reads unstable host state, model it as a probe that produces a snapshot.
If multiple modules need stable access, model it as a facade.
If an interaction writes host state, model it as a mutation scope or command applier.
If a host object can be invalidated, never store it without an invalidation path.

preferred_patterns: hook handlers that publish internal events
facades returning snapshots or safe handles
avoid_patterns: domain services storing raw game objects
UI code mutating host objects directly
global access to private host state

recipes.name: Design a host integration boundary
recipes.when: Use when a feature needs to read or mutate external game state.
steps: Classify each host interaction as notification, read, write, or surface.
Wrap reads as probes or facades returning snapshots.
Wrap writes as gated mutation scopes or command appliers.
Expose readiness and failure diagnostics for each fragile interaction.

pitfalls: Treating a patch callback as a stable domain API.
Passing live host objects through the whole application.
Hiding reflection failure behind default values.
Making mutation boundaries impossible to audit.
review_checklist: Are hooks, probes, facades, and mutations clearly separated?
Can missing host integration be diagnosed?
Can domain logic run from snapshots or contracts?
Are external writes gated and auditable?

references.title: Harmony Patching
references.interpretation: Use when hooks participate in the game boundary.
references.why: Patch callbacks should be converted into stable internal events.

references.title: Reflection Probe Degradation
references.interpretation: Use when host state is discovered through fragile or private APIs.
references.why: Game boundary design must include degraded behavior for fragile probes.
